title: Schedule & Network Diagrams
summary: How project schedules are drawn and analysed — passes, paths, and the diagram styles behind them.

sections.heading: Forward pass, backward pass & critical path
sections.body:
A schedule network links activities by dependency. Two passes find the schedule:

- Forward pass (left to right): compute Early Start (ES) and Early Finish (EF) — the soonest each activity can happen.
- Backward pass (right to left): compute Late Start (LS) and Late Finish (LF) — the latest each can happen without delaying the project.

Total float = LS − ES. The critical path is the longest path through the network; its activities have zero float, so any slip there slips the whole project.
sections.tip: Critical path = longest duration path = zero float. Float on an activity means it can slip that much without moving the end date.

sections.heading: Activity-on-arrow (AOA)
sections.body: An older network style: numbered circles are events (points in time) and arrows are the activities between them. Where a dependency exists but no real work does, a dashed 'dummy' activity is used to keep the logic correct.
sections.tip: AOA shows activities ON the arrows and uses dummy activities (dashed, zero duration) purely for logic.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='52' y1='76' x2='138' y2='52' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><polygon points='131,49 140,51 133,57' fill='currentColor'/><line x1='52' y1='90' x2='138' y2='116' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><polygon points='131,110 140,117 130,119' fill='currentColor'/><line x1='162' y1='52' x2='250' y2='76' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><polygon points='243,71 252,76 242,80' fill='currentColor'/><line x1='162' y1='116' x2='250' y2='90' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><polygon points='242,87 252,90 243,96' fill='currentColor'/><line x1='150' y1='60' x2='150' y2='108' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.3' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><circle cx='40' cy='83' r='14' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5' fill='none'/><circle cx='150' cy='46' r='14' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5' fill='none'/><circle cx='150' cy='122' r='14' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5' fill='none'/><circle cx='264' cy='83' r='14' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5' fill='none'/><text x='40' y='87' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>1</text><text x='150' y='50' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>2</text><text x='150' y='126' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>3</text><text x='264' y='87' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>4</text><text x='90' y='56' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='10'>A</text><text x='90' y='112' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='10'>B</text><text x='208' y='56' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='10'>C</text><text x='208' y='112' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='10'>D</text><text x='158' y='88' fill='currentColor' font-size='8'>dummy</text></svg>
figure.caption: Circles = events; arrows = activities; dashed = dummy (logic only).

sections.heading: Precedence diagramming (AON)
sections.body: The modern style used by most tools: boxes are activities and arrows show the dependency between them. Four relationship types exist — Finish-to-Start (FS, the default), Start-to-Start (SS), Finish-to-Finish (FF), and Start-to-Finish (SF, rare).
sections.tip: AON puts activities IN the nodes. Finish-to-Start is by far the most common dependency.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><rect x='16' y='58' width='56' height='40' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><rect x='126' y='16' width='56' height='40' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><rect x='126' y='100' width='56' height='40' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><rect x='232' y='58' width='56' height='40' rx='4' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><text x='44' y='82' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>A</text><text x='154' y='40' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>B</text><text x='154' y='124' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>C</text><text x='260' y='82' fill='currentColor' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>D</text><line x1='72' y1='72' x2='126' y2='40' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><polygon points='118,37 127,39 120,45' fill='currentColor'/><line x1='72' y1='84' x2='126' y2='118' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><polygon points='118,112 127,119 117,121' fill='currentColor'/><line x1='182' y1='36' x2='232' y2='70' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><polygon points='224,65 233,70 223,74' fill='currentColor'/><line x1='182' y1='120' x2='232' y2='86' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><polygon points='223,82 233,86 224,92' fill='currentColor'/><text x='96' y='50' fill='#1F5FA8' font-size='9'>FS</text></svg>
figure.caption: Boxes = activities; arrows = dependencies (FS shown).

sections.heading: PERT / three-point estimate
sections.body:
Rather than a single guess, PERT uses three estimates — Optimistic (O), Most likely (M), and Pessimistic (P) — and weights the most likely value heavily.

Expected duration E = (O + 4M + P) / 6. The spread (P − O) / 6 gives the standard deviation, a measure of risk in the estimate.
sections.tip: Memorise E = (O + 4M + P) / 6. The 4× weight on Most Likely is what makes it a weighted (beta) average.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 320 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='30' y1='150' x2='300' y2='150' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.4'/><path d='M50,150 C95,150 110,46 138,46 C176,46 235,150 290,150' stroke='#1F5FA8' stroke-width='2' fill='none'/><line x1='60' y1='150' x2='60' y2='138' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><line x1='138' y1='150' x2='138' y2='48' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><line x1='180' y1='150' x2='180' y2='96' stroke='#B5462F' stroke-width='1.4' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><line x1='280' y1='150' x2='280' y2='138' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'/><text x='60' y='166' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>O</text><text x='138' y='166' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>M</text><text x='180' y='166' fill='#B5462F' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>E</text><text x='280' y='166' fill='currentColor' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>P</text><text x='160' y='28' fill='currentColor' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>E = (O + 4M + P) / 6</text></svg>
figure.caption: Optimistic, Most likely, Pessimistic — E is the weighted expected value.

sections.heading: Gantt chart (with milestone)
sections.body: A bar chart of the schedule: one row per activity, each bar spanning its start and finish across a time axis. Bars can show dependencies, and a diamond marks a milestone — a significant zero-duration point such as a go-live or approval.
sections.tip: A milestone has zero duration (a diamond, not a bar). Gantt charts show timing clearly but dependencies less so than a network diagram.

figure.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 320 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='86' y1='20' x2='86' y2='160' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='160' y1='20' x2='160' y2='160' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.6' stroke-dasharray='2 4'/><line x1='234' y1='20' x2='234' y2='160' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='0.6' stroke-dasharray='2 4'/><text x='10' y='42' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>Design</text><text x='10' y='70' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>Build</text><text x='10' y='98' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>Test</text><text x='10' y='126' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>Deploy</text><rect x='88' y='32' width='80' height='14' rx='3' fill='#1F5FA8'/><rect x='150' y='60' width='96' height='14' rx='3' fill='#1F5FA8'/><rect x='196' y='88' width='70' height='14' rx='3' fill='#1F5FA8'/><rect x='240' y='116' width='52' height='14' rx='3' fill='#1F5FA8'/><polygon points='292,142 300,150 292,158 284,150' fill='#B5462F'/><text x='278' y='153' fill='#B5462F' font-size='9' text-anchor='end'>Go-live</text></svg>
figure.caption: Bars span each task's dates; the red diamond is a milestone.

sections.heading: Work breakdown structure (WBS)
sections.body: A hierarchical decomposition of the project's total scope into smaller, manageable pieces. The lowest level is the work package — small enough to estimate and control. The WBS shows scope (the 'what'), not sequence or timing.
sections.tip: The WBS is decomposition of SCOPE, not a schedule. The lowest level is the work package; the 100% rule means children fully add up to their parent.

source: PM Cram study notes — reworded for the current ECO-based PMP exam.
